length_cm.description: Major axis of elliptical top (longest horizontal span, front view width)

depth_cm.description: Minor axis of elliptical top (shortest horizontal span, side view width)

height_cm.description: Overall table height from floor to top surface

top_thick_cm.description: Thickness of the tabletop slab (stone/marble tops typically 3-4 cm)

pedestal_dia_cm.description: Diameter of the single central pedestal column

sanity_checks.rule: length_cm must be greater than depth_cm (oval, not round)
sanity_checks.message: If length_cm equals depth_cm the table is round, not oval. Use round_pedestal_table skill instead. If length_cm < depth_cm the axes are swapped — swap them.

sanity_checks.rule: Aspect ratio must exceed 1.2:1 to justify oval classification
sanity_checks.message: If length:depth ratio is less than 1.2, classify as round_pedestal_table rather than oval_pedestal_table.

sanity_checks.rule: pedestal_dia_cm must be smaller than depth_cm
sanity_checks.message: A pedestal wider than the short axis of the table would protrude beyond the tabletop edge. Reduce pedestal_dia_cm or increase depth_cm.

sanity_checks.rule: height_cm must be greater than top_thick_cm
sanity_checks.message: Table height must be taller than the tabletop thickness alone; the pedestal column needs room below the top.

sanity_checks.rule: pedestal_dia_cm must be smaller than length_cm
sanity_checks.message: Pedestal diameter cannot exceed the table's major axis length. Reduce pedestal_dia_cm.

classification_notes.vs_round_pedestal_table: Use oval when length:depth > 1.2. Use round when length == depth (or ratio < 1.2).
classification_notes.vs_rectangular_table: Oval has curved edges in top view. Rectangle has sharp 90-degree corners.
classification_notes.vs_asymmetric_pedestal_table: Oval has ONE central pedestal. Asymmetric has TWO offset pedestals of different sizes.
